description:
You are acting as a deployment engineer who has just received instructions to roll-out a small in-place configuration update for an application that is already staged in  
/home/user/deployment  

1. BACK-UP (must happen first – automated tests will fail if any original file is overwritten before it is copied):  
   • Create a new directory exactly named /home/user/deployment/backup  
   • Copy the two existing files  
     /home/user/deployment/configs/app.env  
     /home/user/deployment/configs/db.env  
     into the backup directory, preserving their original filenames.  
   • Do NOT move or rename the originals; the originals must stay in …/configs/ after the copy is complete.

2. MODIFY CONFIGURATION FILES in-place inside /home/user/deployment/configs/ using pure text-processing tools (sed/awk/grep, etc.).  
   The required final contents are:

   a) /home/user/deployment/configs/app.env  
      APP_NAME=SampleApp  
      APP_VERSION=1.4.0  
      APP_PORT=8080  

   b) /home/user/deployment/configs/db.env  
      DB_NAME=sampledb  
      DB_HOST=db.internal  
      DB_PORT=5432  
      DB_USER=sampleuser  
      DB_PASSWORD=samplepass  

   Notes:  
   • Only the shown keys may appear (no duplicates, extra whitespace, or comments).  
   • Keys must keep their original on-disk order except for the updated value.  
   • Line-endings must be Unix LF.

3. RELEASE NOTES: create /home/user/deployment/release_notes.txt exactly in this 5-line format (no additional blank lines):

Release Version: 1.4.0  
Release Date: 2024-01-15  
Changes:  
- Updated Application version from 1.3.4 to 1.4.0  
- Changed database host from localhost to db.internal  

4. STRUCTURED LOG: generate /home/user/deployment/update_log.json.  
   The file must be valid single-object JSON (no trailing commas, tabs, or comments).  
   Exact required content:

{
  "update_id": "deploy-001",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T12:00:00Z",
  "files_updated": [
    {
      "file": "app.env",
      "changes": {
        "APP_VERSION": {
          "old": "1.3.4",
          "new": "1.4.0"
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "file": "db.env",
      "changes": {
        "DB_HOST": {
          "old": "localhost",
          "new": "db.internal"
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "backup_directory": "/home/user/deployment/backup"
}

5. VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS (checked automatically):  
   • /home/user/deployment/backup must exist and contain byte-for-byte copies of the pre-update app.env and db.env.  
   • /home/user/deployment/configs/app.env and db.env must match the exact post-update contents listed above.  
   • /home/user/deployment/release_notes.txt and /home/user/deployment/update_log.json must exist and be exactly identical to the formats and values shown.  
   • File ownership and permissions should remain at their defaults (the agent does not need sudo).

Do all processing strictly from the command line; do not open an interactive editor.  
Use the quickest text-processing pipeline you can devise (combining commands with “&&”, pipes, etc.) while assuring the formatting constraints above are met.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

1. Directory structure:
/home/user/deployment/configs          (already exists)
/home/user/deployment/configs/app.env  (contains the 3 lines below)
/home/user/deployment/configs/db.env   (contains the 6 lines below)

2. File contents:

/home/user/deployment/configs/app.env
APP_NAME=SampleApp
APP_VERSION=1.3.4
APP_PORT=8080

/home/user/deployment/configs/db.env
DB_NAME=sampledb
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=sampleuser
DB_PASSWORD=samplepass

No other files or directories named in the public description exist yet.

Expected state after successful completion:

1. New directory:
/home/user/deployment/backup            (must exist)

2. Backup copies inside that directory:
/home/user/deployment/backup/app.env    (exact byte-for-byte copy of the original)
/home/user/deployment/backup/db.env     (exact byte-for-byte copy of the original)

3. Updated files in /home/user/deployment/configs exactly matching:

/home/user/deployment/configs/app.env
APP_NAME=SampleApp
APP_VERSION=1.4.0
APP_PORT=8080

/home/user/deployment/configs/db.env
DB_NAME=sampledb
DB_HOST=db.internal
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=sampleuser
DB_PASSWORD=samplepass

4. Newly-created file:
/home/user/deployment/release_notes.txt  (exact 5-line block as shown)

/home/user/deployment/release_notes.txt
Release Version: 1.4.0
Release Date: 2024-01-15
Changes:
- Updated Application version from 1.3.4 to 1.4.0
- Changed database host from localhost to db.internal

5. Newly-created JSON log:
/home/user/deployment/update_log.json with the exact 37 lines (including braces, indentation, spacing, and newline at EOF) shown in the public description.

The test harness will verify:
• Existence and path spelling of all files/directories.  
• sha256sum equality of backup vs. originals taken *before* modification.  
• sha256sum equality of modified config files against the expected text shown above.  
• sha256sum equality of release_notes.txt and update_log.json against the expected exact strings above.
